---
title: 'NMF-RE: Empirical Illustrations'
author: 'Kenichi Satoh'
date: "Last updated: `r format(Sys.time(), '%Y/%m/%d')`"
output:
  word_document:
    fig_width: 12
    fig_height: 12
    toc: no
---

This script reproduces Tables 4, 5, 7, 8 and Figures 1--4
of "Wild Bootstrap Inference for Non-Negative Matrix Factorization
with Random Effects" (Satoh, 2026).

## Setup
```{r, dpi=300, eval=TRUE, echo=TRUE, warning=FALSE, error=FALSE, message=TRUE}
# ---- Core NMF-RE algorithm ----
source("nmfre_core.R")

# ---- Package dependencies ----
# install.packages("nmfkc")  # CRAN v0.6.0 (used for nmfkc.normalize, nmfkc.DOT)
library(nmfkc)
```




# Example 1: Orthodont (Tables 4--5, Figure 1)
```{r, dpi=300, eval=TRUE, echo=TRUE, warning=FALSE, error=FALSE, message=TRUE}
library(nlme)
print(Sys.time())
rd <- utils:::.getHelpFile(help("Orthodont"))
cat(tools::Rd2txt(rd))

d <- Orthodont
subjects <- unique(d$Subject)
ages <- sort(unique(d$age))
N <- length(subjects)
P <- length(ages)

Y <- matrix(d$distance, nrow = P, ncol = N,
            dimnames = list(paste0("age", ages), subjects))
M <- matrix(ifelse(d$Sex=="Male",1,0), nrow = P, ncol = N,
            dimnames = list(paste0("age", ages), subjects))
male <- as.vector(M[1,])

A <- rbind(
  intercept = rep(1, N),
  male      = male
)

Q0 <- 1

scan <- scan_dfU_cap_rate(1:10/10, Y, A, Q0)
scan$cap.rate

res <- nmf.re(Y, A, Q = Q0, dfU_cap_rate = 0.21, prefix = "Trend")
summary(res)


boxplot(cbind(t(res$B),t(res$B.blup)),col=1:Q0+1)

print(round(res$X,4))
colSums(res$X)
matplot(res$X,type="l",col=1:Q0+1)
legend("topleft",legend=1:Q0,fill=1:Q0+1)


pdf("Figure1.pdf", height = 7, width = 10)
par(mar = c(4,4,1,1) + 0.1, cex = 1.2)
age <- c(8, 10, 12, 14)
plot(age, res$XB[,1], type = "n", ylim = range(Y),xlab = "Age", ylab = "Distance")
for (j in 1:27) {
  pch_j <- ifelse(male[j] == 1, 4, 1)
  points(age, Y[,j], pch = pch_j, col = "black", cex = 0.8)
  lines(age, res$XB.blup[,j], col = "black", lty = 3, lwd = 1.5)
  lines(age, res$XB[,j], col = "black", lwd = 3)
}
legend("topleft",legend = c("Fixed effect","Fixed effect + Random effect (BLUP)",
                  "Male", "Female"),
       lwd = c(3, 1.5, NA, NA),lty = c(1, 3, NA, NA),pch = c(NA, NA, 4, 1),
       col = "black",pt.cex = 1,bg = "white",cex = 0.9)
dev.off()

```




# Example 2: HolzingerSwineford1939 (Tables 7--8, Figure 2)
```{r, dpi=300, eval=TRUE, echo=TRUE, warning=FALSE, error=FALSE, message=TRUE}
library(lavaan)
data(HolzingerSwineford1939)
print(Sys.time())
rd <- utils:::.getHelpFile(help("HolzingerSwineford1939"))
cat(tools::Rd2txt(rd))


d <- HolzingerSwineford1939
dim(d)
index <- complete.cases(d)
d <- d[index,]
dim(d)

colnames(d)
table(d$grade)
table(d$sex)
d$age.rev <- -(d$ageyr+d$agemo/12)
d$sex.2 <- ifelse(d$sex==2,1,0)
d$school.GW <- ifelse(d$school=="Grant-White",1,0)
d[,c("id","sex","ageyr","agemo","school","grade")] <- NULL

d <- nmfkc::nmfkc.normalize(d)

exogenous_vars <- c("age.rev", "sex.2","school.GW")
endogenous_vars <- setdiff(colnames(d), exogenous_vars)
Y <- t(d[, endogenous_vars])
A <- t(d[, exogenous_vars])

Q0 <- 3

scan <- scan_dfU_cap_rate(8:10/10, Y, A, Q0)
scan$cap.rate

res <- nmf.re(Y, A, Q = Q0, dfU_cap_rate = 0.96, prefix = "Factor")
summary(res)


boxplot(cbind(t(res$B),t(res$B.blup)),col=1:Q0+1)


library(rsvg)
library(DiagrammeRsvg)
pngfile <-  paste0("NMF-RE-HS1939.png")
pdffile <- paste0("Figure2.pdf")
res.dot <- nmfkc.DOT(res,threshold = 0.05,type="YXA",rankdir="TB",fill=FALSE,
                     Y.title = "Observation(Y)",X.title = "Factor(X)",
                     A.title = "Covariate(A)")
svg_txt <- DiagrammeRsvg::export_svg(DiagrammeR::grViz(res.dot))
rsvg::rsvg_png(charToRaw(svg_txt),file = pngfile ,width =1000,height = 1000)
rsvg::rsvg_pdf(charToRaw(svg_txt), file = pdffile)
knitr::include_graphics(pngfile)
```




# Example 3: OD Data Hub Model (Figure 3)
```{r, dpi=300, eval=TRUE, echo=TRUE, warning=FALSE, error=FALSE, message=TRUE}
# https://www.e-stat.go.jp/stat-search/files?page=1&stat_infid=000040170612
library(httr)
url <- "https://www.e-stat.go.jp/stat-search/file-download?statInfId=000040170612&fileKind=0"
GET(url, write_disk(tmp <- tempfile(fileext=".xlsx")))
library(readxl)
d <- as.data.frame(read_xlsx(tmp, sheet=1, skip=2))
# Filter for specific flow type
d <- d[d[,1]=="1" & d[,3]=="02" & d[,5]!="100" & d[,7]!="100", ]
pref <- unique(d[,6])

# Create OD Matrix Y (47x47)
U <- matrix(NA, nrow=47, ncol=47)
colnames(U) <- rownames(U) <- pref
d[,5] <- as.numeric(d[,5]); d[,7] <- as.numeric(d[,7])
for(i in 1:47) for(j in 1:47) U[i,j] <- d[which(d[,5]==i & d[,7]==j), 9]
U <- log(1 + U) # Log transformation

dim(U)
colnames(U)
pref_en <- c(
  "Hokkaido", "Aomori", "Iwate", "Miyagi", "Akita", "Yamagata", "Fukushima",
  "Ibaraki", "Tochigi", "Gunma", "Saitama", "Chiba", "Tokyo", "Kanagawa",
  "Niigata", "Toyama", "Ishikawa", "Fukui", "Yamanashi", "Nagano", "Gifu",
  "Shizuoka", "Aichi", "Mie", "Shiga", "Kyoto", "Osaka", "Hyogo", "Nara",
  "Wakayama", "Tottori", "Shimane", "Okayama", "Hiroshima", "Yamaguchi",
  "Tokushima", "Kagawa", "Ehime", "Kochi", "Fukuoka", "Saga", "Nagasaki",
  "Kumamoto", "Oita", "Miyazaki", "Kagoshima", "Okinawa"
)
colnames(U) <- pref_en
rownames(U) <- pref_en
hub.index <- c(13, 23, 27, 40)
colnames(U)[hub.index]

Y <- U[-hub.index,];dim(Y)
A <- U[hub.index,];dim(A)
Q0 <- nrow(A)

scan <- scan_dfU_cap_rate(1:10/100, Y, A, Q0)
scan$cap.rate

res <- nmf.re(Y, A, Q = Q0, dfU_cap_rate = 0.02, prefix = "Region")
summary(res)


boxplot(cbind(t(res$B),t(res$B.blup)),col=1:Q0+1)

B.cluster <- apply(res$B.prob,2,which.max)


pdf("Figure3.pdf", height = 7, width = 10)
par(mar = c(1,1,1,1) + 0.1, cex = 1.2)
library(NipponMap)
st <- paste(hub.index,collapse ="-")

mycol <- c("white","gray70","gray90","gray80")
jp <- JapanPrefMap(col=mycol[B.cluster],
                   main =NULL,border="gray60")
rownames(jp) <- colnames(U)
ad <- matrix(c(0,-2,0,+3,0,-2,0,3),ncol=2,byrow=T)
jpad <- jp[hub.index,]+ad
points(jpad-ad,cex=0.8,pch=19)
for(i in 1:Q0){
  lines(rbind(jpad[i,],jpad[i,]-ad[i,]))
}
text(x=jpad[,1],y=jpad[,2],rownames(jpad),cex=1.2,pos=4)
legend(x=144,y=35, legend=colnames(res$X),
       fill=mycol)
dev.off()



```




# Example 4: Topic Model (Figure 4)
```{r, dpi=300, eval=TRUE, echo=TRUE, warning=FALSE, error=FALSE, message=TRUE}
library(quanteda)
print(Sys.time())

# Load cached corpus for reproducibility (run save_inaugural.R first)
if (file.exists("data/inaugural_corpus.RData")) {
  load("data/inaugural_corpus.RData")
  corp <- corpus(inaugural_texts, docvars = inaugural_docvars)
  cat("Loaded cached inaugural corpus (", length(inaugural_texts), " documents)\n")
} else {
  cat("WARNING: Using quanteda::data_corpus_inaugural directly.\n")
  cat("  For exact reproducibility, run: source('save_inaugural.R')\n")
  corp <- corpus(data_corpus_inaugural)
}
tok <- tokens(corp)
tok <- tokens_remove(tok, pattern=stopwords("en", source="snowball"))
df <- dfm(tok)
df <- dfm_select(df, min_nchar=3)
df <- dfm_trim(df, min_termfreq=100)
d <- as.matrix(df)
# Sort by frequency
index <- order(colSums(d), decreasing=TRUE)
d <- d[,index]

Y <- t(d)
dim(Y)

# Era classification:
# Early period: Independence to Civil War (1775-1865)
# Industrial period: Post-Civil War to WWII (1865-1945)
# Postwar period: Post-WWII to present (1945-)
A <- rbind(
  pre1865   = ifelse(corp$Year <= 1865, 1, 0),
  y1865_45  = ifelse(corp$Year > 1865 & corp$Year <= 1945, 1, 0),
  after1945 = ifelse(corp$Year > 1945, 1, 0)
)
colnames(A) <- colnames(Y)
dim(Y)

Q0 <- 3

scan <- scan_dfU_cap_rate(1:10/100, Y, A, Q0)
scan$cap.rate

res <- nmf.re(Y, A, Q = Q0, dfU_cap_rate = 0.02, prefix = "Topic")
summary(res)


print(round(res$C,4))
print(round(res$X,4))
boxplot(cbind(t(res$B),t(res$B.blup)),col=1:Q0+1)


pdf("Figure4.pdf", height = 7, width = 10)
par(mar = c(8,4,1,1) + 0.1, cex = 1.0)
mycol <- c("white","gray80","gray90")
barplot(res$B.prob,col=mycol,legend=F,las=3,border="gray70",cex.names=0.8,
        ylab="Topic proportion")
legend(x=60,y=0.3,legend=colnames(res$X),bg="white",fill=mycol)
dev.off()


# Topic membership probabilities
Xp <- res$X / rowSums(res$X)
for(q in 1:Q0){
  message(paste0("----- Featured words on Topic [", q, "] -----"))
  print(paste0(rownames(Xp), "(",round(100*Xp[,q], 1), "%)")[Xp[,q]>=0.5])
}

par(mar=c(5,4,4,2)+0.1)
```
